passage_title: AC or DC: The War of Currents

sentences.sentence: Electricity can be delivered in either alternating current (AC), or direct current (DC), and in the late 1880s in America, with electricity delivery in its infancy, it initially seemed clear which system was superior.
sentences.translation: 电力既可以以交流电（AC）形式输送，也可以以直流电（DC）形式输送；在19世纪80年代末的美国，电力输送尚处于起步阶段，起初似乎很清楚哪种系统更为优越。

structure.text: Electricity can be delivered in either alternating current (AC), or direct current (DC)
and in the late 1880s in America
with electricity delivery in its infancy
it initially seemed clear
which system was superior

difficult_points: either A, or B 表示“要么A，要么B”，此处指两种输电方式。
with electricity delivery in its infancy 是 with 复合结构作伴随状语，说明当时电力输送尚处于起步阶段。
it 是形式主语，seemed clear 后接 which 引导的从句作真正的逻辑主语。

sentences.sentence: Thomas Edison, a home-grown American inventor, heavily favoured DC from the start.
sentences.translation: 托马斯·爱迪生，一位土生土长的美国发明家，从一开始就大力支持直流电。

structure.text: Thomas Edison
a home-grown American inventor
heavily favoured DC from the start

difficult_points: a home-grown American inventor 是对 Thomas Edison 的同位语说明。
favour DC from the start 表示从一开始就倾向、支持直流电。

sentences.sentence: Yet the limitations of his system would become increasingly obvious, as would the advantages of AC, and despite Edison's best efforts, his crusade would ultimately be lost.
sentences.translation: 然而，他的系统的局限性会变得越来越明显，交流电的优势也是如此；尽管爱迪生竭尽全力，他的这场斗争最终还是以失败告终。

structure.text: Yet the limitations of his system would become increasingly obvious
as would the advantages of AC
despite Edison's best efforts
his crusade would ultimately be lost

difficult_points: as would the advantages of AC 是倒装结构，完整形式为 as the advantages of AC would become increasingly obvious，用以避免重复。
despite 引导让步状语，despite Edison's best efforts 表示“尽管爱迪生竭尽全力”。
his crusade 比喻爱迪生为推广直流电所做的努力/斗争。

sentences.sentence: In 1879, Edison's team at Menlo Park had improved the electric light bulb, but Edison needed an efficient electricity distribution system to capitalise on this.
sentences.translation: 1879年，爱迪生在门洛帕克的团队改良了电灯泡，但爱迪生需要一套高效的电力分配系统来利用这一成果。

structure.text: Edison's team at Menlo Park had improved the electric light bulb
but Edison needed an efficient electricity distribution system
to capitalise on this

difficult_points: had improved 使用过去完成时，强调这一改良发生在需要配电系统之前。
to capitalise on this 是不定式短语作目的状语，this 指代电灯泡的改良成果。

sentences.sentence: Thus, in 1880, he founded the Edison Illuminating Company and constructed a generating station providing 110 volts of direct current.
sentences.translation: 因此，1880年，他创立了爱迪生照明公司，并建造了一座提供110伏特直流电的发电站。

structure.text: Thus, in 1880
he founded the Edison Illuminating Company
and constructed a generating station
providing 110 volts of direct current

difficult_points: founded 和 constructed 是并列谓语，共用主语 he。
providing 110 volts of direct current 是现在分词短语，修饰 a generating station。

sentences.sentence: Yet such a system has drawbacks.
sentences.translation: 然而，这样的系统也存在缺陷。

structure.text: Yet
such a system
has drawbacks

difficult_points: such a system 指代前一句提到的110伏特直流发电系统。
Yet 表转折，引出下文对该系统缺陷的说明。

sentences.sentence: Due to the low voltage, there Hows correspondingly higher current, meaning that the electrical resistance of the transmission wires significantly reduces the voltage as it travels further afield.
sentences.translation: 由于电压低，相应就会有更高的电流通过，这意味着输电线的电阻会随着电流传输得越远而显著降低电压。

structure.text: Due to the low voltage
there Hows correspondingly higher current
meaning that the electrical resistance of the transmission wires significantly reduces the voltage
as it travels further afield

difficult_points: Hows 应为 flows 的排印错误（原文如此保留），意为“流动”，there flows higher current 即“有更高的电流流过”。
meaning that ... 是现在分词短语作结果状语，说明前述现象带来的后果。
it 指代 the voltage（电压/电流），travels further afield 指其向更远处传输。

sentences.sentence: Whatever thickness of wire is used, there is a natural limitation in the distance over which the electricity can be economically transmitted.
sentences.translation: 无论使用多粗的电线，电力能够经济地传输的距离都存在一个天然的限制。

structure.text: Whatever thickness of wire is used
there is a natural limitation in the distance
over which the electricity can be economically transmitted

difficult_points: Whatever 引导让步状语从句，表示“无论……”。
over which 引导定语从句修饰 the distance，over 与 distance 前后呼应。

sentences.sentence: There are, however, benefits to using DC.
sentences.translation: 然而，使用直流电也有其好处。

structure.text: There are
however
benefits to using DC

difficult_points: however 是插入语，表示语气转折，承接上一段对直流电缺陷的描述。
benefits to doing sth 表示“做某事的好处”。

sentences.sentence: It can allow storage batteries to be directly connected to the electricity grid, giving extra power to meet sudden short-term peaks of demand, or backup during breakdown of supply.
sentences.translation: 它可以让储能电池直接接入电网，从而在需求出现突发的短期高峰时提供额外电力，或在供电中断时提供备用电力。

structure.text: It
can allow storage batteries to be directly connected to the electricity grid
giving extra power to meet sudden short-term peaks of demand
or backup during breakdown of supply

difficult_points: It 指代前一句提到的 DC（直流电）。
allow sb/sth to do sth 结构表示“让……做……”。
giving extra power... 是现在分词短语作结果状语，说明储能电池接入电网带来的作用。

sentences.sentence: Furthermore, during Edison's time, there were no practical AC motors, only DC ones.
sentences.translation: 此外，在爱迪生所处的时代，还没有实用的交流电动机，只有直流电动机。

structure.text: Furthermore, during Edison's time
there were no practical AC motors
only DC ones

difficult_points: ones 代替 motors，避免重复。
only DC ones 是对前半句的补充对比，强调当时只有直流电动机可用。

sentences.sentence: Also, most of the load consisted of incandescent light bulbs, which ran well on DC.
sentences.translation: 此外，大部分用电负荷来自白炽灯泡，而白炽灯泡在直流电下运行良好。

structure.text: Also
most of the load consisted of incandescent light bulbs
which ran well on DC

difficult_points: consist of 表示“由……构成”。
which 引导定语从句修饰 incandescent light bulbs，说明其在直流电下的表现。

sentences.sentence: Perhaps most importantly, Edison had the patents (legal rights) to many associated DC devices which he and his team had invented, such as meters, telegraphic devices, and household machinery.
sentences.translation: 或许最重要的是，爱迪生拥有他和他的团队所发明的许多相关直流电设备的专利（法律权利），比如电表、电报设备和家用机械。

structure.text: Perhaps most importantly
Edison had the patents
(legal rights)
to many associated DC devices
which he and his team had invented
such as meters, telegraphic devices, and household machinery

difficult_points: 括号内容是对 patents 的同位解释，即“法律权利”。
which 引导定语从句修饰 DC devices，说明这些设备是爱迪生及其团队发明的。
such as ... 列举 DC devices 的具体例子。

sentences.sentence: Thus, the widespread adoption of DC across America would see him gain considerably from patent royalties.
sentences.translation: 因此，直流电在美国的广泛采用会让他从专利版税中获得可观的收益。

structure.text: Thus
the widespread adoption of DC across America
would see him gain considerably from patent royalties

difficult_points: see sb do sth 表示“使某人做某事、导致某人经历某事”，此处 the widespread adoption... 作主语。
patent royalties 指因专利授权获得的版税收入。

sentences.sentence: Still, all such inventions were somewhat useless when DC electricity could only be delivered to customers within a few kilometers of the generating source.
sentences.translation: 尽管如此，当直流电只能输送给发电站几公里范围内的用户时，所有这些发明就显得有些无用了。

structure.text: Still
all such inventions were somewhat useless
when DC electricity could only be delivered to customers within a few kilometers of the generating source

difficult_points: Still 在此表转折，意为“尽管如此”，承接上一段爱迪生因专利获利的描述。
when 引导时间状语从句，说明这些发明“无用”所处的具体限制条件。

sentences.sentence: To overcome this problem, the best answer is to transform, or step-up, the voltage to very high levels for transmission, and then transform it down to safe levels for customer use.
sentences.translation: 为了解决这个问题，最好的办法是把电压升压到非常高的水平以便传输，然后再将其降压到对用户来说安全的水平。

structure.text: To overcome this problem
the best answer is to transform, or step-up, the voltage to very high levels for transmission
and then transform it down to safe levels for customer use

difficult_points: or step-up 是对 transform 的同义替换说明，即“升压”。
it 指代 the voltage；transform it down 与前面的 transform ... to very high levels 形成对比（升压/降压）。

sentences.sentence: This also allows thinner and less expensive wires, but there is no low-cost technology to transform voltage — unless one uses AC, and it was the brilliant physicist and prolific inventor, Nikola Tesla, who had extensively researched this system.
sentences.translation: 这样做还能使用更细、更便宜的电线，但当时并没有低成本的变压技术——除非使用交流电，而正是杰出的物理学家兼多产发明家尼古拉·特斯拉，对这一系统进行了广泛的研究。

structure.text: This also allows thinner and less expensive wires
but there is no low-cost technology to transform voltage
unless one uses AC
and it was the brilliant physicist and prolific inventor, Nikola Tesla, who had extensively researched this system
who had extensively researched this system

difficult_points: This 指代前一句提到的升压降压方案。
unless one uses AC 引导条件状语从句，说明低成本变压技术的例外情况。
it was ... who ... 是强调句结构，突出 Nikola Tesla 这一人物。

sentences.sentence: Tesla, a penniless immigrant from Serbia, worked for a year at Edison's Menlo lab.
sentences.translation: 特斯拉，一位来自塞尔维亚、身无分文的移民，曾在爱迪生的门洛实验室工作过一年。

structure.text: Tesla
a penniless immigrant from Serbia
worked for a year at Edison's Menlo lab

difficult_points: a penniless immigrant from Serbia 是对 Tesla 的同位语说明，交代其早期背景。
worked for a year 明确说明特斯拉在爱迪生实验室工作的时长，为下文的冲突和离职做铺垫。

sentences.sentence: He had actually proposed the AC system to Edison, but Edison, an empirical experimenter with little formal education, dismissed it as impractical.
sentences.translation: 他其实曾向爱迪生提出过交流电系统的构想，但爱迪生——一个几乎没受过正规教育、凭经验做实验的人——却认为这不切实际，予以否定。

structure.text: He had actually proposed the AC system to Edison
but Edison
an empirical experimenter with little formal education
dismissed it as impractical

difficult_points: had actually proposed 用过去完成时，强调这一提议发生在被否定之前。
an empirical experimenter with little formal education 是对 Edison 的同位语说明，暗示其经验主义、非科班出身的特点。
dismiss ... as impractical 表示“认为……不切实际而予以否定”。

sentences.sentence: Tesla, with the mathematical training and formal theoretical knowledge, was able to understand AC's potential, even inventing an AC polyphase electric induction motor.
sentences.translation: 特斯拉凭借数学训练和正规的理论知识，能够理解交流电的潜力，甚至发明了交流多相感应电动机。

structure.text: Tesla
with the mathematical training and formal theoretical knowledge
was able to understand AC's potential
even inventing an AC polyphase electric induction motor

difficult_points: with the mathematical training and formal theoretical knowledge 说明特斯拉具备的知识背景，与前一句爱迪生的经验主义形成对比。
even inventing ... 是现在分词短语作结果状语，进一步说明其理解交流电潜力的具体成果。

sentences.sentence: Tesla soon felt he was not being given due credit or enough financial compensation from Edison, and a direct confrontation led to him immediately resigning, after which he was reduced to working as a labourer for a few years to make ends meet.
sentences.translation: 特斯拉很快感觉到自己没有从爱迪生那里得到应有的认可或足够的经济补偿，一次正面冲突导致他立即辞职；此后他不得不沦落到做几年劳工来维持生计。

structure.text: Tesla soon felt
he was not being given due credit or enough financial compensation from Edison
and a direct confrontation led to him immediately resigning
after which he was reduced to working as a labourer for a few years
to make ends meet

difficult_points: felt 后省略 that，he was not being given... 是宾语从句。
led to him immediately resigning 中 him 是动名词 resigning 的逻辑主语。
after which 引导非限定性定语从句，which 指代前面的“正面冲突导致他辞职”这一事件。
be reduced to doing sth 表示“沦落到做某事”，语气带有落差感。

sentences.sentence: But Tesla was not the first to advocate AC.
sentences.translation: 但特斯拉并不是第一个提倡交流电的人。

structure.text: But Tesla
was not the first
to advocate AC

difficult_points: the first to do sth 表示“第一个做某事的人”。
But 承接上一段结尾提到的特斯拉，转折说明他并非交流电最早的倡导者。

sentences.sentence: The system was being atrialed in many European countries, with considerable success.
sentences.translation: 这套系统当时正在许多欧洲国家进行试用，并取得了相当大的成功。

structure.text: The system was being atrialed in many European countries
with considerable success

difficult_points: atrialed 疑为 trialled/trialed（试用、试验）的排印错误，此处按原文保留。
with considerable success 是伴随状语，说明试用的结果。

sentences.sentence: One of the converts to the cause was a university-trained electrical engineer named George Westinghouse, and he was willing to invest in the idea.
sentences.translation: 这一事业的皈依者之一是一位受过大学教育的电气工程师，名叫乔治·威斯汀豪斯，他愿意为这个想法投资。

structure.text: One of the converts to the cause
was a university-trained electrical engineer
named George Westinghouse
and he was willing to invest in the idea

difficult_points: convert 在此作名词，意为“皈依者、转而支持者”，指转而支持交流电的人。
named George Westinghouse 是过去分词短语，修饰 electrical engineer，说明其姓名。

sentences.sentence: He formed a company and purchased the patents to AC-based transformer technology from its European inventors, as well those to Tesla's AC polyphase electric motor, among others.
sentences.translation: 他成立了一家公司，从欧洲发明者手中购买了交流变压器技术的专利，还购买了特斯拉交流多相电动机等其他专利。

structure.text: He formed a company
and purchased the patents to AC-based transformer technology from its European inventors
as well those to Tesla's AC polyphase electric motor
among others

difficult_points: as well those 之间原文缺少 as，完整应为 as well as those，此处按原文保留。
those 代替 the patents，避免重复。
among others 表示“在其他专利之中”，说明所购专利不止此处列举的这些。

sentences.sentence: This eventually led to him hiring Tesla himself to help commercialise AC, and promote it as a better system.
sentences.translation: 这最终促使他雇用了特斯拉本人，帮助将交流电商业化，并将其宣传为一种更好的系统。

structure.text: This eventually led to him hiring Tesla himself
to help commercialise AC
and promote it as a better system

difficult_points: led to him hiring ... 中 him 是动名词 hiring 的逻辑主语。
to help commercialise AC 和 promote it as a better system 是并列的不定式短语，说明雇用特斯拉的目的。

sentences.sentence: A bitter feud, known as the 'War of Currents' was set to begin.
sentences.translation: 一场被称为“电流之战”的激烈争斗即将拉开序幕。

structure.text: A bitter feud
known as the 'War of Currents'
was set to begin

difficult_points: known as the 'War of Currents' 是过去分词短语，修饰 A bitter feud，原文在该短语后缺少逗号，此处按原文保留。
be set to do sth 表示“即将、注定要做某事”。

sentences.sentence: Edison first strike was to claim that high-voltage systems were too dangerous to use.
sentences.translation: 爱迪生的第一记打击，就是宣称高压系统太危险而不宜使用。

structure.text: Edison first strike
was to claim
that high-voltage systems were too dangerous to use

difficult_points: Edison first strike 原文缺少所有格撇号 s，完整应为 Edison's first strike，此处按原文保留。
too ... to ... 结构表示“太……而不能……”。

sentences.sentence: Certainly they were dangerous, but Westinghouse countered that such risks could be minimised and were considerably outweighed by the benefits.
sentences.translation: 这些系统确实是危险的，但威斯汀豪斯反驳说，这些风险是可以被降到最低的，而且相较于其带来的好处，这点风险完全是次要的。

structure.text: Certainly they were dangerous
but Westinghouse countered
that such risks could be minimised
and were considerably outweighed by the benefits

difficult_points: they 指代前一句的 high-voltage systems。
that 引导的宾语从句中，could be minimised 与 were... outweighed 是并列谓语。
be outweighed by 表示“被……超过/胜过”，即风险的分量小于好处。

sentences.sentence: Edison's next strike was to use his influence on various American state governments to limit power transmission to low voltages, effectively eliminating AC from the competition.
sentences.translation: 爱迪生的下一记打击，是利用他对美国各州政府的影响力，将输电限制在低电压范围内，从而有效地把交流电排除在竞争之外。

structure.text: Edison's next strike was to use his influence on various American state governments
to limit power transmission to low voltages
effectively eliminating AC from the competition

difficult_points: to limit power transmission to low voltages 是不定式短语，说明利用影响力的目的。
effectively eliminating AC from the competition 是现在分词短语作结果状语，说明这一做法产生的效果。

sentences.sentence: When this failed, Edison was prepared to conduct public electrocutions of animals by AC — even on a rogue elephant no longer wanted by its circus owners.
sentences.translation: 当这一招失败后，爱迪生准备用交流电对动物进行公开电击处决——甚至包括一头不再被马戏团主人所需要的、行为失控的大象。

structure.text: When this failed
Edison was prepared to conduct public electrocutions of animals by AC
even on a rogue elephant
no longer wanted by its circus owners

difficult_points: this 指代前一句提到的限制输电电压的做法。
even on a rogue elephant 是对 animals 的举例补充，even 强调极端程度。
no longer wanted by its circus owners 是过去分词短语，修饰 a rogue elephant，说明这头大象被马戏团抛弃的原因。

sentences.sentence: In the battle of public opinion, this was even filmed.
sentences.translation: 在这场舆论战中，这一幕甚至被拍摄了下来。

structure.text: In the battle of public opinion
this was even filmed

difficult_points: this 指代前一句提到的电击大象事件。
even 强调这一做法的极端程度，甚至被拍摄记录下来用于宣传。

sentences.sentence: The next logical step was to show AC's deadliness on human beings themselves.
sentences.translation: 下一步顺理成章的举动，就是要展示交流电对人体本身的致命性。

structure.text: The next logical step
was to show AC's deadliness on human beings themselves

difficult_points: themselves 用于强调 human beings 本身，而非此前的动物。
was to show 与前一段爱迪生对动物做电击实验形成递进关系，此处目标转向人类本身。

sentences.sentence: Edison, realising that he was losing the war, again used his influence on government, this time to promote the use of AC for the execution of prisoners.
sentences.translation: 爱迪生意识到自己在这场战争中已渐落下风，便再次动用他对政府的影响力，这一次是为了推动使用交流电来处决囚犯。

structure.text: Edison
realising that he was losing the war
again used his influence on government
this time to promote the use of AC for the execution of prisoners

difficult_points: realising that he was losing the war 是现在分词短语作伴随状语，说明爱迪生此举的心理动机。
this time 与前文（限制输电电压）形成对比，强调这是另一次不同目的的行动。

sentences.sentence: Thus, in 1890, the first 'electric chair' was constructed in anticipation of an impending death sentence.
sentences.translation: 因此，1890年，第一把“电椅”被建造出来，以备执行即将到来的死刑判决。

structure.text: Thus, in 1890
the first 'electric chair' was constructed
in anticipation of an impending death sentence

difficult_points: in anticipation of 表示“为……做准备、预期到……”。
impending death sentence 指即将执行的死刑判决。

sentences.sentence: Westinghouse countered by hiring the best lawyers of the day to defend the prisoner in question, as well as to prevent the system of execution.
sentences.translation: 威斯汀豪斯的反制之策，是雇用当时最好的律师为涉案囚犯辩护，同时也试图阻止这种处决方式的推行。

structure.text: Westinghouse countered
by hiring the best lawyers of the day
to defend the prisoner in question
as well as to prevent the system of execution

difficult_points: by doing sth 表示“通过做某事（的方式）”，说明威斯汀豪斯反制的具体手段。
to defend... 与 to prevent... 是并列的不定式短语，说明雇用律师的双重目的。

sentences.sentence: Although he failed in both respects, the results were unexpected.
sentences.translation: 尽管他在这两方面都以失败告终，但结果却出人意料。

structure.text: Although he failed in both respects
the results were unexpected

difficult_points: both respects 指代前一句提到的辩护囚犯和阻止处决这两个目标。
unexpected 引出下文对具体“意外结果”的说明。

sentences.sentence: Despite a botched execution and the horror of the spectators, the electric chair would remain, but AC would not be stigmatised as the killer Edison's had hoped.
sentences.translation: 尽管这次处决执行得很糟糕，围观者也感到恐惧，但电椅还是被保留了下来；不过，交流电并没有像爱迪生所希望的那样被污名化为“杀手”。

structure.text: Despite a botched execution and the horror of the spectators
the electric chair would remain
but AC would not be stigmatised as the killer
Edison's had hoped

difficult_points: botched execution 指处决过程执行失败、出现纰漏。
Edison's had hoped 中 Edison's 疑为排印错误，完整应为 Edison had hoped（爱迪生曾希望），此处按原文保留。
be stigmatised as 表示“被污名化为”，与爱迪生的期望形成对比，暗示他的策略最终失败。

sentences.sentence: Meanwhile, AC's range and efficiency saw Westinghouse being given high prestige engineering projects, such as the Ames Hydro-electric Generating Plant (1891), and another one on Niagara Falls, culminating in the greatest public relations victory in 1893: the contract to illuminate the Columbian Exposition in Chicago.
sentences.translation: 与此同时，交流电的传输范围和效率优势，使威斯汀豪斯获得了多个享有盛誉的工程项目，比如1891年的埃姆斯水力发电厂，以及尼亚加拉瀑布的另一个项目，并在1893年迎来了公关上最大的胜利：为芝加哥哥伦布纪念博览会提供照明的合同。

structure.text: Meanwhile
AC's range and efficiency
saw Westinghouse being given high prestige engineering projects
such as the Ames Hydro-electric Generating Plant (1891), and another one on Niagara Falls
culminating in the greatest public relations victory in 1893
the contract to illuminate the Columbian Exposition in Chicago

difficult_points: see sb being given sth 表示“使某人获得……”，AC's range and efficiency 作主语，具有拟人化的因果意味。
culminating in ... 是现在分词短语作结果状语，说明这些工程项目最终达到的高峰成就。
冒号后的内容是对 the greatest public relations victory 的具体说明。

sentences.sentence: Here, Tesla and Westinghouse showed the wonders of AC power with various electrical exhibits, such as fluorescent lamps and Tesla's AC motors, to an awestruck audience and widespread press attention.
sentences.translation: 在这里，特斯拉和威斯汀豪斯借助各种电气展品，比如荧光灯和特斯拉的交流电动机，向惊叹不已的观众展示了交流电的神奇之处，也引起了媒体的广泛关注。

structure.text: Here
Tesla and Westinghouse showed the wonders of AC power
with various electrical exhibits
such as fluorescent lamps and Tesla's AC motors
to an awestruck audience and widespread press attention

difficult_points: Here 指代前一句提到的哥伦布纪念博览会现场。
with various electrical exhibits 说明展示交流电奇迹所使用的具体手段。
to an awestruck audience and widespread press attention 说明展示的效果/对象，press attention 与 audience 并列作 to 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: After that, the war was effectively won, and AC would take over almost completely.
sentences.translation: 在那之后，这场战争实际上已经获胜，交流电几乎完全占据了主导地位。

structure.text: After that
the war was effectively won
and AC would take over almost completely

difficult_points: that 指代前一句提到的哥伦布纪念博览会展示这一事件。
take over 表示“接管、占据主导地位”，指交流电最终取代直流电成为主流。
